REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GREGORY JONES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT EXISTS AS TO THE DATE OF ACCRUAL OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION AND PLAINTIFF'S DILIGENCE IN FINDING/CONFIRMING THAT A CAUSE OF ACTION EXISTS. CONTRARY TO THE DEFENDANT'S ASSERTION, UNLIKE IN MANEFEE, THIS COMPLAINT IS NOT BASED ON A FACIALLY DEFECTIVE NOTICE. NOTHING IN THE COMPLAINT SUGGESTS THAT THE NOTICE WAS IN ANY WAY DEFECTIVELY DRAFTED OR SERVED. TENANT'S DILIGENCE IN CONFIRMING LANDLORD'S ALLEGED SUBJECTIVE WRONGFUL INTENT AND SUBSEQUENT VIOLATION (E.G. FAILURE TO TIMELY MOVE-IN OR TO STAY FOR REQUISITE PERIOD OF TIME) IS AN ISSUE OF FACT TO BE DETERMINED BY THE JURY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Off Calendar Jul-09-2020 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Df
Off Calendar Jul-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Daine Prichard, 415.398.0200 ext. 230 for Defendant Gregory Jones.
Kristen Drake, 888.510.7511 ext. 101

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT GREGORY JONES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Triable issue of fact exists as to the date of accrual of the cause of action and Plaintiff's diligence in finding/confirming that a cause of action exists. Contrary to the Defendant's assertion, unlike in Manefee, this complaint is not based on a facially defective Notice. Nothing in the Complaint suggests that the Notice was in any way defectively drafted or served. Tenant's diligence in confirming Landlord's alleged subjective wrongful intent and subsequent violation (e.g. failure to timely move-in or to stay for requisite period of time) is an issue of fact to be determined by the jury. Prevailing party to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
